claim: Reports best-frontier-LM accuracy on the Hard subset ranging from 28.7% to 61.5% across 45 regions.
Documented under-performance on questions about North Africa, South America, and Middle East.
1,696 human-written and human-verified questions covering 45 global regions.
Constructs an evaluation suite of 197,243 QA pairs from local exam sources to measure capabilities of multilingual LLMs.
Comprehensive knowledge- and reasoning-centric benchmark across 44 written languages.
LLMs exhibit zero-shot geospatial prediction with Spearman rho up to 0.89 for factual variables.
Bias against locations with lower socioeconomic conditions on subjective topics with Spearman rho up to 0.70.
Multilingual and Arabic monolingual LMs exhibit bias towards entities associated with Western culture.
Introduces CAMeL framework for measuring cultural bias.
